title: Fix Page Search Score=0.0 Under-Ranking
description: Research Report R1-013 (Confidence 91%, High). Pages returned from MemorySmith search have Score=0.0, causing them to be systematically under-ranked compared to memory records — even when the page content is more relevant. This misleads planners and LLMs relying on search ordering.\\n\\nRoot Cause (confirmed by code audit 2026-06-26):\\n- MemorySmith.SearchController.cs:53 — pages get `Score=null` from `UnifiedSearchResult`\\n- PageSummary model (PageService.cs:216) has NO Score property at all\\n- Sorting: `.OrderByDescending(result => result.Score ?? 0)` pushes pages to bottom\\n- Agent side: RestMemoryGateway.SearchAsync faithfully maps null→0.0 via `h.Score ?? 0.0`\\n- SearchMemoryTool ignores Score entirely — just uses first result as `bestPageId`\\n\\nFix approach (two-tier):\\n\\n**Tier 1 (Agent-side, within scope):**\\n1. In RestMemoryGateway.SearchAsync (line ~46), assign a minimum score to page results:\\n   - Pages: Score = max(0.05, existing score) so they aren't pushed to absolute bottom\\n   - Memories: preserve original score\\n2. In SearchMemoryTool, when building result dictionary, include Score per result\\n3. Optionally: scan page results for coordinate patterns too (like memory results)\\n\\n**Tier 2 (Cross-repo — MemorySmith server):**\\n1. Add Page relevance scoring to MemorySmith (lexical BM25 or semantic)\\n2. `PageSummary` record needs a `double? Score` property\\n3. Sort unified results with a fair interleaving algorithm instead of score-only\\n\\nCross-repo request should be filed under MS-Requests.\\n\\nFiles:\\n- Agent.Memory/RestMemoryGateway.cs:34-47 (SearchAsync)\\n- Agent.Tools/Tools/SearchMemoryTool.cs:48-91 (ExecuteAsync)\\n- MemorySmith/MemorySmith.App/Controllers/SearchController.cs:26-61 (API)\\n- MemorySmith/MemorySmith.App/Services/PageService.cs:216 (PageSummary)\\n\\nSource: research-MemorySmith.Agent Sprint-35 Code Audit, Council Addendum 2026-06-26. Code audit: SteveBot 2026-06-26.,\\n  \"type\": \"Task\",\\n  \"status\": \"Ready\",\\n  \"priority\": \"High\",\\n  \"assigneeMode\": \"Custom\",\\n  \"assigneeDirectoryId\": null,\\n  \"assigneeCustomText\": \"Agent\",\\n  \"reporter\": \"anonymous\",\\n  \"labels\": [\\n    \"sprint-51\",\\n    \"council\",\\n    \"memory-gateway\",\\n    \"search\",\\n    \"ranking\"\\n  ],\\n  \"attachments\": [],\\n  \"externalLinks\": [],\\n  \"linkedPages\": [],\\n  \"comments\": [],\\n  \"epicId\": null,\\n  \"parentId\": null,\\n  \"dueDateUtc\": null,\\n  \"createdAtUtc\": \"2026-06-27T00:33:14.717734Z\",\\n  \"updatedAtUtc\": \"2026-06-27T00:33:14.717734Z\",\\n  \"completedAtUtc\": null,\\n  \"revision\": 1,\\n  \"isArchived\": false,\\n  \"hasLoadError\": false,\\n  \"loadError\": null,\\n  \"sourceFilePath\": null\\n}\\n

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
Council review priority update (2026-06-29): Elevated to High priority. This task (page search score=0.0 under-ranking) is a prerequisite for completion-driven autonomy. When the LLM evaluator searches for context during autonomy evaluations, blueprint pages ranked at 0.0 will be invisible. Must be resolved before TSK-0240 (Autonomy Phase 2) begins.

See: `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Retrieval Specialist finding.
